author: @forlornfool221
text: 😢❤RIP

author: @TheMrRatzz
text:
This didn't look like a man who was "bored", and thought his projects were "repetitive".

I can't watch these without wondering what was going on behind that confidence and smile.😢

replies.author: @yasirrakhurrafat1142
replies.text:
I know... I felt quite uncertain about the reasoning he gave, about the last video he made.
Being a kind man, I think he didn't want to bluntly admit his struggles, even if his own.. to avoid us from being hurt or concerned about him.
Although, I'm more hurt that he didn't voice his struggles.
But all that I can remember him by would be how he presumed the goodness in people, how hopeful of a man he was, and his philosophy.
He mattered a great deal.. to me when times were tough, his hopefulness was contagious, and any negative reflections and thoughts would start to escape my mind.. when I would hear his discoveries and his teachings, which he always exhibited with great enthusiasm and pride.
Nothing I can ever say would be sufficient to truly communicate how good of a man he was.
All I can say is.. he did radiate throughout his life.. a light of hope, and he shined bright. And I believe many have seen, observed and absorbed that light, which we will carry with immense pride.. to have ever known him.

Love you Sir Robert Murray-Smith, you had been amazing, may you accept the highest comfort in heaven.

replies.author: @yasirrakhurrafat1142
replies.text:
Hope you have a lovely day.
And, as he would have never wanted to induce any negativity amongst any of his learners.. I wish that you can get over the sorrow of his passing. And rather, become more resilient, strong and kind, and proud of being one of his learners.

author: @bhelpurii
text: RIP good sir

author: @DM-kl4em
text: It's a terrible injustice that 3D printing did not yet exist while Da Vinci was alive.  I'm also old enough to remember Freddy the Freeloader on the Red Skelton Show (the episode that guest-starred actor Vincent Price).  "This says it was painted by ... Lee-ya-narda ... da vinky."

author: @GabrielPettier
text:
Well, you could easily give it more oompf by making the two arms a bit thicker, and maybe join them with a spring in compression, but then of course you are deviating from the design, and there are certainly many ways to improve it.

Nice little toy. Your prints are always nice quality and strong, i need to learn to give a build the plastic it needs 😅

author: @littleworkshopofhorrors2395
text: The flaw in his design is where you stand to pull the trigger, I maybe a coward but I wouldn't want that arm heading in my direction.  A better design would to trigger release with a side mounted cam or even a push rod from the "safe" side. A cam would allow a better "feel" when firing I feel.😂

text: Look at the real life size one I built last year! Turned it into a cheese curd catapult for a local french association, super fun project. Used wooden springs but had to supplement with rubber springs..! Made it to the national news with it 😂
